chat.title: Chat
chat.emptyDescription: Send a message to get started.
chat.emptySetup: Connect an AI provider to unlock your assistant.
chat.noAssistantsDescription: Create one or finish setup to start chatting.
chat.setupDialogDescription: Choose a model to start chatting with this assistant.

setup.title: Get started
setup.description: Connect your AI, pick a model, and start chatting.
setup.providerDescription: Choose how the app talks to AI models.
setup.modelDescription: Select the model your assistant will use.
setup.agentDescription: Name your assistant and go to chat.
setup.providerStepDescription: Add the connection your assistants will use. You can add more later.
setup.serviceUrlHint: Leave blank if the provider uses its default endpoint.
setup.modelStepDescription: Choose the model that will answer for this assistant.
setup.discoveredModelsHint: Click Use to register and select a model.
setup.noRegisteredModels: No models registered yet. Discover models or enter an ID manually.
setup.agentStepDescription: Name the assistant, then open chat.
setup.currentAssistantHint: The selected model will be attached to the current assistant.

toasts.noModelsReturned: No models returned by this provider
toasts.modelsDiscovered: {count, plural, one {# model discovered} other {# models discovered}}

agents.title: Assistants
agents.description: Create and tune assistants for your team.
agents.name: Name

model.systemPromptPlaceholder: Describe role, tone, and limits for this assistant…
model.promptSuggestionsPlaceholder:
Summarize this document in 5 points
Draft a reply email
List the next actions
model.promptSuggestionsHint: One suggestion per line. A few will appear on the empty chat screen.
model.advancedHint: Sampling, memory, guardrails, and tool behavior

agents.configureDescription: Name, model, instructions, tools, and knowledge.

configurePage.identityHint: How this assistant appears in chat and the list.
configurePage.modelHint: Which AI powers replies and how it should behave.
configurePage.setupDescription: Do the minimum now. Tools, knowledge, and advanced settings can wait.
configurePage.lockedDescription: Everyone can use it, but only admins can edit it. Clone it to create your own version.
configurePage.cloneToEditHint: Clone this assistant to edit your own copy without changing the organization version.
configurePage.builtinToolsHint: Platform tools this assistant can call.
configurePage.mcpToolsHint: External tools connected via MCP servers.
configurePage.noMcpToolsSynced: No tools synced for this server.

list.pageDescription: Manage your AI assistants — each one can have its own model, instructions, tools, and knowledge bases.
list.guideDescription: Create the assistant, choose the AI that replies, then test it in chat. Tools and knowledge stay optional.
list.needsSetupCount: {count, plural, one {# to finish} other {# to finish}}
list.configuredCount: {count, plural, one {# assistant configured} other {# assistants configured}}
list.emptyDescription: Create your first assistant to start chatting with AI. Each assistant gets its own model, instructions, tools, and knowledge bases.
list.deleteDescription: This permanently removes the agent and its configuration versions. This action cannot be undone.
list.createNextDescription: You’ll go straight to the assistant page to choose its model, logo, and capabilities.

support.name: Support assistant
support.description: Answers customer questions with a clear, calm, solution-oriented tone.
support.prompt: You are a customer support assistant. Answer clearly, ask questions when needed, suggest concrete next steps, and flag when a request should be handed to a human.

suggestions.0: Help me reply to this customer
suggestions.1: Summarize this ticket and suggest next steps

hr.name: HR assistant
hr.description: Helps teams understand procedures, internal policies, and HR documents.
hr.prompt: You are an internal HR assistant. Explain policies carefully, state the limits of your knowledge, and recommend contacting HR for sensitive or personal cases.

suggestions.2: List the HR points to check

documents.name: Document assistant
documents.description: Analyzes, summarizes, and compares documents or knowledge bases.
documents.prompt: You are a document analysis assistant. Summarize information, cite available sources, separate facts from assumptions, and point out missing context.

sales.name: Sales assistant
sales.description: Drafts emails, offers, customer replies, and commercial summaries.
sales.prompt: You are a B2B sales assistant. Help write concise, contextual messages with clear next steps and a professional tone.

suggestions.1: Turn these notes into a commercial proposal
suggestions.2: Prepare a reply to this objection

project.name: Project manager
project.description: Turns discussions into action plans, decisions, and operational follow-up.
project.prompt: You are a project management assistant. Clarify goals, break work into steps, identify risks/dependencies, and suggest dated actions when useful.

suggestions.0: Create an action plan from these notes

blank.name: Custom assistant
blank.description: Start blank and define the assistant’s role freely.
blank.prompt: You are a helpful, clear, and careful assistant. Adapt your answers to the user’s needs.

capabilities.customToolsHint: Tools you created. They are only visible to you.
capabilities.skillsHint: Markdown instructions installed from skills.sh. No Python files or scripts are attached to assistants.

providers.title: AI connections
providers.description: Connect to AI providers and manage available models for your assistants.
providers.setupDescription: Finish setup before configuring AI connections.
providers.advancedHint: Provider type, auth, and custom headers

manager.heroDescription: Add the connection, discover models, then choose them in your assistants. Technical settings stay in Advanced.
manager.modelsShown: {count, plural, one {# model shown} other {# models shown}}
manager.systemHealthHint: Status, model counts, and connection details
manager.configuredConnections: {count, plural, one {# connection configured} other {# connections configured}}
manager.noConnectionsHint: Connect an AI to make its models available to assistants.
manager.selectProvider: Select a connection to manage its models.
manager.manualModelHint: Use only if discovery does not find the model you need.
manager.noModels: No models registered yet. Click Discover models to start.
manager.selectProviderDetails: Select a connection to view details.
manager.addDialogDescription: Enter only the name, URL, and API key. Everything else is optional.
manager.keepCurrentKey: Leave blank to keep current key…
manager.archiveDescription: The connection will be archived. Existing assistant versions may keep references to its models.
manager.removeModelDescription: The model will be removed from this connection. Assistants already bound to it may need reconfiguration.
manager.errorConnectProvider: Unable to connect to the AI service. Check the URL and API key.
manager.toastDiscoveredModels: {count, plural, one {# model discovered} other {# models discovered}}

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Upload documents and attach them to assistants so they can reference your data.
knowledge.guideDescription: Create a base, add documents, then attach it to an assistant from the assistant page. Test search stays optional.
knowledge.selectedDocumentsCount: {count, plural, one {# document here} other {# documents here}}
knowledge.attachDialogTitle: Attach this base to an assistant
knowledge.errorAttachAgent: Failed to attach base to assistant
knowledge.optionalSearchHint: Optional: check what an assistant will find in this base.
knowledge.name: Name
knowledge.basesDescription: Choose the source set to manage.
knowledge.emptyBasesDescription: Create a base to upload documents for your assistants.
knowledge.selectBaseDescription: Choose or create a base on the left to manage documents.
knowledge.documentsHint: Paste text to index. Processing documents refresh automatically.
knowledge.dropHint: Drag and drop a text file here to ingest

tools.title: Tools & integrations
tools.description: Built-in tools, MCP servers, and approval queue.

builtin.title: Built-in tools
builtin.description: Native capabilities your assistants can call during conversations. Enable them per assistant from the Agents page.
builtin.noResultsHint: Try another keyword or clear the category filter.

tools.mcpHelp: External tools connected via the Model Context Protocol.
tools.skillsHelp: Install Markdown-only agent skills from skills.sh and attach them to assistants.
tools.approvalsHelp: Review tool calls that need human approval.

mcp.advancedHint: Custom HTTP headers and environment variables

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.description: Discover, install, and share assistants, skills, and tools with your team.

share.title: Share “{name}”

steps.meta: Customize the listing, then it goes live immediately.

share.existingDraft: An existing draft will be updated.

fields.name: Name
fields.releaseNotesPlaceholder: e.g. new tools added, updated instructions…

publish.title: Marketplace
publish.description: Customize, then publish immediately to the marketplace.

user.title: User
user.description: Private share with one selected teammate.

secrets.warning: If you include credentials, the installer won’t need to reconfigure anything. Only authorized people can use them.

list.emptyShared: Nothing has been shared with you yet

toast.credentialsNeeded: Add connection credentials to finish setup.
Add connection credentials to finish setup.

visibility.publicHint: Visible to everyone in the marketplace

settings.title: Settings
settings.languageDescription: Choose the interface language for your account.
settings.adminLinkDescription: Registration, automation, integrations, and system health are managed separately.

admin.platformSettingsDescription: Registration, automation, integrations, and system health.

registration.title: Registration
registration.description: Control whether people can create accounts without an admin.
registration.hint: The first account can always be created and becomes admin. After that, this setting decides public sign-up access.

sidebarNavigation.title: Sidebar navigation
sidebarNavigation.description: Reorder navigation items and choose which pages appear in the sidebar for everyone.
sidebarNavigation.hint: Drag items to reorder them. Choose whether each page appears in the main sidebar, Planning, or in the collapsible Advanced section. Disabled items stay available by direct URL but are hidden from the sidebar. Admin-only pages still respect role permissions.

assistantGovernance.title: Assistant governance
assistantGovernance.description: Set the organization order, default assistant, and promoted assistants for everyone.
assistantGovernance.status: {count, plural, =0 {No assistants} one {# assistant} other {# assistants}}
assistantGovernance.policyDescription: The Assistants page stays focused on usage and personal preferences. Global decisions — order, default, and team assistant management — live here.
assistantGovernance.adminOnlyDescription: Only admins can change the organization order and default assistant.
assistantGovernance.emptyDescription: Mark an assistant as global or recommended to include it in organization governance.
assistantGovernance.defaultHint: Used when users first open chat, unless they chose their own default.
assistantGovernance.orderDescription: This order is used in the chat selector and organization assistant lists.

chatAutomation.title: Chat automation
chatAutomation.description: Choose the small model used for conversation titles and next-message suggestions.
chatAutomation.enableDescription: When disabled, chat falls back to simple local titles.
chatAutomation.disabledDescription: Provider, model, and suggestion settings stay hidden until this feature is enabled.
chatAutomation.saveInvalid: Select a provider and model before enabling automation.
chatAutomation.noProvidersDescription: Create an AI connection before enabling chat automation.

customToolBuilder.title: Custom tool builder
customToolBuilder.description: Configure the LLM and n8n MCP used by the custom tool creation page.
customToolBuilder.disabledDescription: LLM, n8n MCP, and tool-name settings stay hidden until this feature is enabled.
customToolBuilder.secretsTitle: Secrets stay out of the model context
customToolBuilder.secretsDescription: The builder can request a secure modal. Submitted values are encrypted server-side and the LLM only receives opaque credential refs.
customToolBuilder.enableDescription: When disabled, the public builder API refuses requests.
customToolBuilder.mcpHint: For the web app, use an SSE or streamable HTTP MCP URL. Stdio configs work for desktop clients but cannot be called by this route.
customToolBuilder.allowActivationDescription: Keep this off to require human review before production activation.

admin.membersDescription: Create accounts, manage admins, and control who has access.

members.inviteDescription: Create or update app accounts from the Users page.
members.emptyDescription: Create a user account to grant app access.

platform.createDescription: Create a sign-in account and grant app access immediately.
platform.name: Name

admin.platformAccountsDescription: Create sign-in accounts, manage admins, and suspend access from one place.
admin.adminRequiredDescription: Only admins can change account and registration settings.
admin.usageDescription: Monitor token consumption, API usage, and quota.

usage.quotaWarning: Approaching or exceeding the configured monthly limit.
usage.tokenChartDescription: Input vs output for the last {count} events.
usage.recentUsageDescription: Newest usage records in the app.

admin.auditDescription: Track security-sensitive actions, changes, and access events.

audit.recentEventsDescription: Security-sensitive actions recorded in the app.
audit.emptyDescription: Try adjusting filters or check back after activity.

admin.apiKeysDescription: Create and manage API keys for external scripts, CI pipelines, and integrations.

apiKeys.cardDescription: Create keys for technical integrations. Use Authorization: Bearer <key> on supported routes like chat.
apiKeys.created: API key created — copy it now, it will not be shown again

customTools.title: Custom tools
customTools.description: Create custom tools with an assistant, preview the workflow as it’s built, and connect secrets without exposing them.

scheduledTasks.title: Scheduled tasks
scheduledTasks.description: Schedule automatic prompts for your assistants without opening the chat page.
scheduledTasks.sectionHint: These tasks run automatically with the selected assistant. Results are posted to a dedicated conversation created on the first run.

create.title: Create a task
create.description: Define the prompt, assistant, and run cadence.

empty.title: No scheduled tasks
empty.description: Create your first task to automate a briefing, report, or recurring follow-up.

noAssistants.title: No assistant available
noAssistants.description: Create an assistant before scheduling an automatic task.
